text.title: django-celery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-celery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Passing a Django model instance to a task via .delay(user=user_obj).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Celery serializes the object at call time. The worker deserializes stale data or fails entirely on restart. Always pass the primary key and fetch fresh inside the task.

failure-mode.description: Calling self.retry() inside a task defined without bind=True.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without bind=True, self is undefined. The task crashes on the first retry attempt with a NameError, not a retry.

failure-mode.description: Defining tasks without soft_time_limit and time_limit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without limits, a hung task (blocked network call, deadlocked DB query) holds a worker slot indefinitely, starving all other tasks in that queue.

failure-mode.description: Running multiple celery beat processes without RedBeat or django-celery-beat for leader election on multi-replica deployments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each beat process fires the full schedule independently. N replicas produce N copies of every periodic task, causing duplicate side effects.

failure-mode.description: Building deeply nested Celery chains/chords on a Redis broker without monitoring back-pressure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Redis-backed chord results accumulate in memory. Deep nesting stalls the entire workflow when intermediate results expire or the broker fills up.

failure-mode.description: Inlining API keys or credentials directly in task function bodies or as default argument values.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Task code is deployed to all workers. Secrets in code appear in logs, tracebacks, and version history. Use environment variables loaded via systemd EnvironmentFile.

failure-mode.description: Dispatching tasks across all users (User.objects.all().iterator()) without an explicit human approval gate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single typo or bug fan-outs to every user. Require an explicit approval checkpoint before any task that sends to more than N users.
